secac__001.text:
Other Content:
Email correspondence with RSS client IC00021
From: Neurotact UK <yaqixie@neurotact.uk>
Sent: Thursday, January 25, 2024 3:12 PM
To: Smith, Claire M <claire.smith@imperial.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: RSS request IC00021 - availability for a meePng with the RSS CTU team
Dear Claire,
Thank you so much for sharing the NHS digital regulations guidance. This is indeed
a precious resource for us.
I appreciate it greatly! This information will be highly beneficial as we navigate the
development and regulatory process.
Best regards,
Yaqi
Yaqi Xie
yaqixie@neurotact.uk
www.neurotact.uk
We Sense What You Feel
Innova1on In Pain Management
Neurotact UK
From: Smith, Claire M <claire.smith@imperial.ac.uk>
Sent: 25 January 2024 14:48
To: Neurotact UK <yaqixie@neurotact.uk>
Subject: RE: RSS request IC00021 - availability for a meePng with the RSS CTU team
Dear Yaki
Susan came across this website hXps://www.digitalregulaPons.innovaPon.nhs.uk/ which has a link to
regulaPons and guidance for developers (hXps://www.digitalregulaPons.innovaPon.nhs.uk/
developers-guidance/all-developers-guidance/). It takes developers through the various steps from
technology idea through to technology in use and updates.
We thought you might find it helpful.
Kind regards
Claire
Claire Smith PhD
Business Development & OperaHons Manager – Clinical Research
Imperial Clinical Trials Unit
Please note my normal working days are Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday (am), Thursday.

From: Neurotact UK <yaqixie@neurotact.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, January 24, 2024 4:36 PM
To: Smith, Claire M <claire.smith@imperial.ac.uk>
Cc: susansmith@qmul.ac.uk; T.HUSSAIN@QMUL.AC.UK
Subject: Re: RSS request IC00021 - availability for a meePng with the RSS CTU team
Dear Claire,
Thank you for the valuable insights and resources shared during our recent meeting.
Your guidance on research justification, team building, regulation, and feasibility
study planning are immensely helpful. I appreciate your advice and will reconsider
the focus of the i4i Connect application as suggested. I will explore collaboration with
a Clinical Trials Unit at the right stage.
Thank you once again for your invaluable support and guidance.
Best regards,
Yaqi
Yaqi Xie
yaqixie@neurotact.uk
www.neurotact.uk
We Sense What You Feel
Innova1on In Pain Management
Neurotact UK
From: Smith, Claire M <claire.smith@imperial.ac.uk>
Sent: 24 January 2024 13:20
To: Neurotact UK <yaqixie@neurotact.uk>
Cc: susansmith@qmul.ac.uk <susansmith@qmul.ac.uk>; T.HUSSAIN@QMUL.AC.UK
<t.hussain@qmul.ac.uk>; Smith, Claire M <claire.smith@imperial.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: RSS request IC00021 - availability for a meePng with the RSS CTU team
Dear Yaki
Thank you for taking the Pme to discuss your research plans with the RSS team from Imperial College
and Partners (me, Tahera and Susan) last week. And thanks to Susan for providing the links during
our discussion. They also appear below for convenience.
Here are a few notes from that discussion:
• It's important to jusHfy why the research is needed:
What is the clinical problem? What is the scale of the problem? How does it impact the NHS / the
paPents? How will your technology address the problem? How will you measure improvement?
• Build your team
NIHR will be looking for a mulPdisciplinary team to support the work. We recommend as a minimum
geeng a clinician on board. Consider approaching the Medtech and In vitro diagnosPcs Co-
operaPves (MICs). hXps://www.nihr.ac.uk/partners-and-industry/industry/access-to-experPse/

medtech.htm. MICs can help you to build a mulP-disciplinary team that will work collaboraPvely with
you to ensure you generate the evidence you need to support adopPon of your medical device,
digital technology or diagnosPc in the NHS. The NIHR Devices 4 Dignity MIC – Delivering innovaPve
technology soluPons to support people with long-term condiPons, preserving their dignity and
independence (devicesfordignity.org.uk) might be parPcularly relevant.
• AI as a Medical Device
You menPoned that your device is currently class I (with no UKCA mark as yet) but once the AI
component is added then it becomes Class 1a. RegulaPon in this area is challenging and will conPnue
to evolve. Here is a link to a guidance document that you may find useful The RegulaPon of ArPficial
Intelligence as a Medical Device (publishing.service.gov.uk)
• Planning a feasibility study
And another link you may find helpful: Guidance on applying for feasibility studies | NIHR
• Focus of i4i Connect applicaHon
Trying to deliver a feasibility study in 12 months and a budget capped at £150k will be very
challenging. Also it may be too soon to embark on a feasibility study if you only have pilot data on 11
volunteers.
Consider changing the focus of the i4i applicaPon to include several work packages, for example (a)
PPI work including the workshops with paPents, family members and carers that you already have in
mind, (b) clinician engagement / forming collaboraPons (see comment above about building the
team) and (c) understanding the regulatory landscape (see comment above about AI as a MD).
• When to engage a Clinical Trials Unit
CTUs offer different levels of support with feasibility studies. Some CTUs will collaborate in full, some
will offer light touch support, e.g. operaPons / staPsPcs advisory roles and a database and some
CTUs don't support feasibility studies. The decision may in some cases be driven by budget
restricPons, for example the Research for PaPent Benefit funding stream Research for PaPent Benefit
| NIHR caps the budget for feasibility studies at £300k. I suggest that when you are ready to start
planning the feasibility study you make a new approach to the RSS to request advice and explore the
opportuniPes to collaborate with a CTU.
We wish you well with the next stage in your product development.
Kind regards
Claire
Claire Smith PhD
Business Development & OperaHons Manager – Clinical Research
Imperial Clinical Trials Unit
Please note my normal working days are Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday (am), Thursday.
